vulnerabilities.title: Same-block partial fills share one order hash and permanently lock remaining assets
vulnerabilities.summary: The protocol derives rental identity from `_deriveRentalOrderHash(order)` and stores only `orders[orderHash] = true` in storage. For Seaport partial fills of the same order, if two fills happen in the same block for the same renter/safe and with the same per-fill amounts, Create builds identical `RentalOrder` structs (same `seaportOrderHash`, items, lender/renter, and `startTimestamp = block.timestamp`), producing the same order hash (`Signer.sol` 162-194, `Create.sol` 578-595). `Storage.addRent

description.desc: Active rentals are tracked by a boolean `orders[orderHash]`; multiple fills that map to the same `orderHash` are collapsed into one storage entry, but stopping deletes that entry after a single settlement/reclaim.

vulnerabilities.impact: Permanent lock of lender assets (notably ERC1155 partial amounts) and corresponding escrowed payments; protocol can no longer execute a valid stop path for the remaining portion once the single boolean order flag is deleted.
vulnerabilities.proof_of_concept:
1) Attacker fulfills a PARTIAL_RESTRICTED BASE/PAY order with numerator/denominator (e.g., 1/2) twice in the same block for the same rental safe.
2) Both fills produce the same protocol rental order hash, so protocol state aggregates asset/payment balances but has only one active `orders[hash]` boolean.
3) When rental is stopped, protocol reclaims/settles only one per-fill amount and then deletes `orders[hash]`.
4) Remaining rented balance stays in the safe with `rentedAssets > 0`, but any second stop reverts `StorageModule_OrderDoesNotExist`, leaving the remainder unrecoverable.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not represent active orders with a single boolean. Track multiplicity per order hash (e.g., `mapping(bytes32 => uint256 activeCount)`) and decrement on each stop, deleting only when the count reaches zero. Alternatively, ensure each fulfillment gets a unique stop key (include a per-fill nonce/counter) so multiple fills cannot collapse into one removable storage slot.
